seoTitle: How to Properly Leave a Slack Workspace
metaDescription: Learn the difference between signing out and deactivating your Slack account, and the steps to permanently leave a workspace without affecting others.
intro: Whether you're wrapping up a client project or leaving a former employer, properly exiting a Slack workspace involves more than just closing the app. This guide explains the difference between simply signing out and formally deactivating your account for a specific workspace, and walks through the account settings you'll need to complete the process without disrupting your other connected teams.

steps.heading: Understand signing out versus deactivating
steps.body: Signing out just logs you out of the app on that device, while deactivating permanently removes your active profile from that specific workspace.

steps.heading: Open your account settings
steps.body: Click your profile avatar in the target workspace, select the three-dot 'More' options menu next to your name, and choose Account Settings.

steps.heading: Navigate to the deactivation module
steps.body: You'll be redirected to your online Slack account panel. Scroll to the bottom of the settings page to find the Deactivate Account section.

steps.heading: Review the warnings and confirm your password
steps.body: Read the details about message retention, then enter your password to confirm you want to proceed with deactivation.

steps.heading: Deactivate your account
steps.body: Click 'Yes, Deactivate My Account' to permanently remove your profile and icon from that workspace's sidebar.

faq.question: Will leaving a workspace delete my sent messages?
faq.answer: Your previously sent messages generally remain visible to other members even after you leave, though your profile will show as deactivated.

faq.question: What if I'm the Primary Owner of the workspace?
faq.answer: Primary Owners must transfer workspace ownership to someone else before they're able to leave or deactivate their account.

faq.question: Does leaving one workspace affect my other Slack workspaces?
faq.answer: No, deactivating your account is specific to that single workspace and won't affect your access to other connected workspaces.

faq.question: Can I rejoin a workspace after leaving?
faq.answer: Yes, but you'll need a fresh invitation from someone in that workspace to be added back in.
